title: [meganium] Battle Guide

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[meganium] is a pure [grass] support wall with balanced bulk (80/100/100) and access to both [light-screen] and [reflect]. Its offense is minimal at 82/83, but that's intentional: [meganium]'s job is to set screens, keep itself alive with [synthesis], and enable setup sweepers on the back line to come in safely. It isn't flashy, but it's reliable.

## Strengths
The dual-screen access is what sets [meganium] apart from other [grass] walls. Few Pokemon in the format can run [light-screen] and [reflect] on the same slot, and the combination halves damage on both sides of the spectrum while [meganium] buffers turns with [synthesis]. [giga-drain] doubles as self-healing and STAB damage, which keeps [meganium]'s HP pool topped up during long exchanges. Overall it functions as a defensive pivot that extends the team's effective HP stat.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[grass] typing is rough — Fire, Ice, Flying, Poison, and Bug all hit for 2x, which means most offensive teams have at least one attacker that threatens [meganium]. Its own damage output is too low to pressure walls, so taunt users neuter it. A fast special attacker using flamethrower or ice-beam is the direct answer to [meganium]'s support plan, and pivoting out at the right time is often the only survival option.

## Recommended Moveset
[giga-drain] is the locked STAB because it heals [meganium] on hit. [light-screen] and [reflect] are both mandatory for the support plan. [synthesis] fills the last slot as the self-heal, though aromatherapy is an alternative if the team has a stacked status problem to solve. Dropping one screen for toxic or leech-seed makes [meganium] more offensive but less of a team supporter.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[leftovers] is the default because it stacks with [synthesis] to extend [meganium]'s turns on field. [miracle-seed] is an alternate that raises [grass] STAB damage. The ability should be [overgrow]. Tera [steel] flips most of the weakness profile into resists, while Tera [water] erases the Fire weakness and provides more defensive synergy.

## Teammates & Synergy
[meganium] exists to enable setup sweepers. A dragon-dance or swords-dance user on the back line converts the screen turns into a free setup, which is the entire point of the screen support. A Fire answer (bulky [water]) covers the biggest structural weakness, and a fast pivot gives [meganium] safe entry turns. A status absorber lets [meganium] stay healthy through toxic chip over a long game.

## Play Patterns
The basic sequence is [light-screen] turn one, [reflect] turn two, then either [synthesis] or [giga-drain] based on HP. Pivot out once a setup sweeper is ready to come in under screens, and save [meganium] for a second round of screens later in the match. [giga-drain] is a last-resort damage slot; don't rely on it to break anything. Save the Tera for the exact moment an Ice or Fire move is about to land.
